notes: Payload begins at the 5' LTR; in 3rd-gen SIN vectors this is a chimeric/truncated LTR where U3 is replaced by a heterologous promoter (RSV or CMV).
Payload ends at the 3' LTR. A ΔU3 deletion (self-inactivating) is a near-definitive marker of modern lentiviral transfer plasmids vs. retroviral.

features: HIV-1 Ψ | HIV-1 psi pack | psi plus pack
notes: HIV-1 packaging signal; essentially pathognomonic for HIV-1-derived lentivirus when combined with LTRs.
Rev Response Element; required for nuclear export of unspliced genomic RNA. Strong lentiviral marker.
Central polypurine tract; improves transduction efficiency. Common in 2nd/3rd-gen lentiviral vectors.
Woodchuck post-transcriptional regulatory element. Boosts transgene expression. Also used in some AAV/retro vectors — not lenti-exclusive.
Truncated gag retained because packaging signal extends into the gag ORF; non-functional but structurally important.

features: 5' LTR -> HIV-1 Ψ -> RRE -> cPPT/CTS -> [transgene cassette] -> WPRE -> 3' LTR
notes: Canonical element order. Matching the full order is essentially diagnostic.
Co-occurrence of these three cis elements between LTRs is nearly exclusive to HIV-1 lentiviral transfer plasmids.
Presence of ITRs argues strongly against lentivirus.
Inverted terminal repeats flank the rAAV cassette. ~145 bp each. Two ITRs in inverted orientation are the defining feature.
Two ITRs in inverted orientation separated by ≤~4.7 kb (AAV packaging limit) is pathognomonic for rAAV transfer plasmid.
WPRE is common in rAAV expression cassettes but also in lenti/retro — weak alone.

features: bGH poly(A) signal | SV40 poly(A) signal | hGH poly(A) signal
notes: Short polyA signals are preferred in rAAV due to the 4.7 kb packaging limit. Weak on its own.

features: Rep | Cap | AAV2 Rep | AAV2 Cap
notes: Rep/Cap present OUTSIDE ITRs indicates a helper/packaging plasmid, not the transfer vector payload. Reclassify as AAV helper plasmid.
LTR/psi presence excludes AAV.
MLV LTRs (~600 bp) flank the provirus; distinct from HIV LTRs in sequence.
MMLV/MESV packaging signal — specific to gamma-retrovirus; not interchangeable with HIV-1 Ψ.
These are lentivirus-specific; their presence rules out gamma-retrovirus.

features: MLV LTR (5') + MMLV Ψ + MLV LTR (3')
notes: MLV LTRs + MMLV Ψ co-occurrence is diagnostic. MSCV and MESV vectors follow the same pattern.
Adenoviral ITRs (~100 bp) flank the genome. Distinguished from AAV ITRs by sequence — both may be annotated simply as 'ITR'.
Adenovirus packaging signal — diagnostic when present.
Adenoviral early genes; often deleted (ΔE1/ΔE3) in gene therapy vectors, so absence does not exclude.
Adenoviral splice acceptor; supporting evidence.

features: HIV-1 Ψ | MMLV Ψ | AAV2 ITR
notes: Other viral packaging elements exclude adenovirus.

features: HSV TK promoter | HSV TK | HSV TK poly(A) signal
notes: HSV TK elements are frequently used as markers in non-HSV vectors too (e.g. for selection). Weak as a standalone HSV indicator.
Engineered TK deletions/mini variants are more specific to HSV amplicon/oncolytic vectors.
HSV VP16 activation domain; used broadly in chimeric TFs (Tet-On/Off, GAL4-VP16). Only weak evidence for HSV payload unless combined with TK/OriS.

features: HSV oriS | HSV oriL | pac site | a sequence
notes: HSV origins of replication and pac cleavage/packaging sites are definitive for HSV amplicons, if annotated.

features: polyhedrin promoter | p10 promoter | p6.9 promoter | basic protein promoter
notes: Very late baculoviral promoters driving heterologous expression. Strong indicator.
Native polh UTRs retained in transfer vectors.

features: baculovirus recombination region (ORF1629) | baculovirus recombination region (lef2/ORF603) | ORF1629 | ORF603 | lef2
notes: Flanking homology arms for recombination into AcMNPV genome (Bac-to-Bac / BaculoDirect). These define the transfer cassette boundaries.
Tn7 transposition arms are used in Bac-to-Bac bacmids; their presence flanking the cassette is nearly diagnostic.

features: gp64 promoter | GP64 signal sequence | GP64 mature domain
notes: gp64 envelope elements; used for baculovirus surface display, supportive.

features: OpIE-1 promoter | OpIE-2 promoter | OpIE-2 poly(A) signal | hr5 enhancer | IE1 promoter | IE0 promoter
notes: Insect-cell-specific promoters/enhancers (OpMNPV, AcMNPV). Seen in both baculoviral transfer plasmids and stable insect expression plasmids.

features: p7.5 promoter | pSynE/L | mH5 promoter | p11 promoter
notes: Vaccinia-specific early/late promoters are diagnostic. Not present in the reference feature file — may require custom annotation.

features: TK flanking region (vaccinia) | HA flanking region (vaccinia)
notes: Homology arms for recombination into the TK or HA locus of the vaccinia genome define the transfer cassette.
T5NT motif terminates vaccinia early transcripts; supporting evidence.

features: LB T-DNA repeat | RB T-DNA repeat
notes: Left and Right Border repeats define the T-DNA; everything between them is transferred to the plant genome. Presence of both is diagnostic.

features: CaMV 35S promoter | CaMV 35S promoter (enhanced) | minimal CaMV 35S promoter | Ubi promoter
notes: Plant-active promoters driving payload expression. Common but not exclusive to T-DNA (also used in particle bombardment constructs).

features: CaMV poly(A) signal | hsp70 poly(A) | nos terminator
notes: Plant-active polyA/terminator; supporting.

features: TMV Ω | AtADH 5'-UTR | OsADH 5'-UTR | BYDV 5' UTR
notes: Plant translation-enhancing 5' UTRs; weak supporting evidence.

features: pVS1 RepA | pVS1 oriV | pBBR1 Rep | pBBR1 oriV | pSa ori
notes: Broad-host-range origins outside the T-DNA support the identification as an Agrobacterium binary vector (backbone replicates in both E. coli and Agrobacterium).

features: LB T-DNA repeat + RB T-DNA repeat + (CaMV 35S promoter | plant selectable marker)
notes: LB+RB flanking a plant-active promoter or selectable marker is diagnostic for a T-DNA payload.
F-plasmid mini-F replicon (repE + partition sopABC) is the defining backbone of BAC vectors (pBeloBAC, pBACe3.6).
Mini-F ori2 is part of the BAC replicon; supporting.
Chloramphenicol resistance is the typical BAC selection marker; very weak alone (generic).
Many BACs include loxP sites for Cre-mediated retrofitting.
A centromere/ARS plus two telomeric repeats is the defining structure of a linear YAC.
Yeast auxotrophic markers; supportive but not specific (also used in 2μ plasmids).
2μ origin argues for an episomal 2μ-based yeast vector rather than a true YAC.
Cohesive-end sites (cos) define the boundaries of lambda DNA that gets packaged into phage heads. Two cos sites 38–52 kb apart is diagnostic.

features: λ repressor | λ repressor (ts) | cI857 LR | λ N peptide
notes: Lambda regulatory proteins; supporting for lambda-derived vectors.

features: λ attP | phage Φ80 attP | HK022 attP | phage 186 attP
notes: Phage attachment sites; used for integration-based cloning.
f1/M13 intergenic region directs ssDNA packaging by helper phage. Common in phagemids but also in many dual-purpose cloning vectors; moderate evidence.

features: M13 gene II | M13 gene V | M13 gene VIII | M13 gene III | M13 gene VI | M13 gene VII | M13 gene IX
notes: Presence of actual M13 structural/replication genes (not just the ori) indicates a full phage genome or phage display vector.
pIII fusion downstream of the payload is the hallmark of M13 phage display.
TMV 5' leader; translation enhancer. Common in plant transient expression including non-viral binary vectors — weak alone.
Used by both viral and non-viral plant vectors; weak.

features: TMV movement protein | TMV coat protein | TMV replicase | PVX TGB | PVX CP
notes: Actual viral replication/movement ORFs indicate a true plant viral replicon.
For IVT-mRNA preceding LNP encapsulation — payload is linearized template. T7 upstream of the ORF is typical.

features: poly(A) signal | bGH poly(A) signal | SV40 poly(A) signal
notes: Generic; little discriminatory power.
Canonical IVT-mRNA cassette layout for LNP delivery.

features: CMV promoter | CMV enhancer | EF-1α promoter | SV40 promoter | CAG promoter
notes: Strong mammalian promoter; ubiquitous in transient expression plasmids. Very weak classifier on its own.
Bacterial selection; generic.

features: ITR | 5' LTR | LB T-DNA repeat | cos | repE
notes: Absence of any specialized packaging/integration element defaults classification to generic transient pDNA.
